subject: Re: Warp Extracting Subtomograms Multiple Times
body:
So ... I've still not found a solution to the problem. Warp keeps 
extracting particles from some tomograms 2x or 3x.

On different machines, different versions 😳

I've attached the input (bin4_test_reduced.star) and output 
(bin4_test_reduced_OUTPUT.star) of a minimal star file (one tomogram), 
where particles are always extracted 2x.

Does anybody spot anything suspicious that could cause such a behavior?

I get the same behavior if I use star files written by me (matlab; 
tom_toolbox) or by Relion (3.x).

Any suggestions would be greatly appreciated! 🙇‍♂️


Philipp.

On Tuesday, October 5, 2021 at 4:34:47 PM UTC+2 3P1L wrote:

> *Dear All,*
>
> I've encountered an interesting behavior of Warp when reconstructing 
> subtomograms:
>
> I go in with a particle list of ~15k particles (from Relion 3.1), out 
> comes a list of ~23k particles.
>
> I guess this is one way to lift up that FSC curve 😜
>
> Apparently, for some of the tomograms particles are extracted 2x and 3x. 
> 🤔 [didn't see an obvious pattern such as this would always happen to the 
> 3rd, 6th, ... or 4th, 8th, ...  tomogram].
>
> The setup:
>
> Warp 1.0.9 on
> Windows 10 Pro 64 Bit, Intel Xeon Gold 6230
> 384 GB RAM, 6xRTX 2080 Ti
>
> Anybody ever seen this?
>
> [I'm currently testing if this happens, too, if I disable some of the 
> GPUs; will report later]
>
> Thanks,
>
> Philipp.
>
>
>
